RESULT OF AGM
Star announces the result of voting on the resolutions at its AGM held today, Thursday 18 June 2026, as set out in the AGM notice.
A poll was held on each of the resolutions and was passed by the required majority. Resolutions 1 to 10 were passed as ordinary resolutions, Resolutions 11 and 12 were passed as special resolutions. The results of the poll were as follows:

193,032,384
shares, which was the total number of shares entitling the holders to attend and vote for or against all resolutions. In accordance with the Company's Articles of Association, on a poll every member has one vote for every share held. Votes withheld are not votes in law and have not been counted in the calculation of the proportion of vote "for" or "against" a resolution. Proxy appointments which gave discretion to the Chairman
have been included in the "for" total.
The poll results will also be posted on the Company's website in due course.

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
28 May 2026 09:43
RNS Number : 0606G
Star Energy Group PLC
28 May 2026
28 May 2026
Star Energy Group plc
("Star Energy" or the "Company")
Director/PDMR Shareholding
Star Energy (AIM:STAR) announces that Ross Glover, Chief Executive Officer, and Frances Ward, Chief Financial Officer, sold an aggregate of 350,016 ordinary shares of 0.002 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") to satisfy tax and national insurance liabilities arising from the exercise of options as announced on 26 May 2026.
Ross Glover has sold 289,541 Ordinary Shares and therefore, following this transaction, he is interested in 845,803 Ordinary Shares representing 0.44% of the issued share capital of the Company.
Frances Ward has sold 60,475 Ordinary Shares and therefore, following this transaction, she is interested in 320,474 Ordinary Shares representing 0.17% of the issued share capital of the Company.
The notification below, made in accordance with the requirements of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 which forms part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, provides further detail.

latest.title: Exercise of Options and Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
26 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5198F
Star Energy Group PLC
26 May 2026
26 May 2026
Star Energy Group plc
("Star Energy" or the "Company")
Exercise of Options and Total Voting Rights
Star Energy (AIM:STAR)
announces that it has been notified that Ross Glover, Chief Executive Officer, and Frances Ward, Chief Financial Officer, have exercised options over an aggregate of 777,812 ordinary shares of 0.002 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at nil cost pursuant to the 2015 Management Retention Plan and the 2016 Executive Incentive Plan (the "Option Shares"). To satisfy the option exercise, 777,812 new Ordinary Shares will be issued ("New Ordinary Shares"). The New Ordinary Shares will be admitted to trading on AIM pursuant to the Company's block listing admission facility announced on 21 May 2026.
Ross Glover has exercised 643,424 Option Shares and therefore, following this transaction, he is interested in 1,125,870 Ordinary Shares representing 0.58% of the issued share capital of the Company.
Frances Ward has exercised 134,388 Option Shares and therefore, following this transaction, she is interested in 371,475 Ordinary Shares representing 0.19% of the issued share capital of the Company.
Following the exercise, Ross Glover and Frances Ward intend to sell Ordinary Shares to cover tax and national insurance liabilities.
Total Voting Rights
The issued share capital of the Company following the above issue of New Ordinary Shares will be 192,642,955 Ordinary Shares; the number of deferred shares
remains 303,305,534.
The total number of voting rights in Star Energy will be 192,642,955 Ordinary Shares, which should be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in Star Energy under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
The notification below, made in accordance with the requirements of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 which forms part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, provides further detail.
Notification and public disclosure of transactions by persons discharging managerial responsibilities and persons closely associated with them.

company_30s.what_they_do: Star Energy Limited builds and operates geothermal power plants, mainly in Europe and the Balkans. They focus on extracting heat from underground reservoirs and converting it into clean electricity for local grids.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Geothermal is a low‑carbon, base‑load renewable that fills a gap left by intermittent sources. Star’s projects tap into untapped heat resources, giving them a niche advantage in regions where other renewables are scarce. Their technology can supply steady power to growing industrial zones without the price swings of wind or solar.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a restructuring phase. It has recently sold a Croatian geothermal asset, raised up to £0.6 m through a retail offer, and is filing a block‑listing application to protect its shares. Cash burn is high but the recent fundraising shows it can keep the lights on while it looks for new projects or partnerships.

rns_narrative: Star is trimming its portfolio by selling a Croatian geothermal operation, freeing capital for new, potentially higher‑yield projects.
A new retail offer and a block‑listing application indicate management is trying to stabilize the share price and protect investors from a flood of sell orders.
Recent disclosures about director shareholdings and option exercises suggest insiders are still committed, which can be a quiet sign of confidence amid the turbulence.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet‑but‑watchful – the community is largely silent, with a handful of cautious comments.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are almost no bullish voices; the few who are optimistic point to the clean‑energy tailwinds and the company’s ability to raise capital.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears cite the heavy cash burn, the need to sell assets, and the uncertainty around future project approvals as red flags.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has shifted from mild optimism about the sale to a more neutral stance as the company’s financials tighten.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat, meaning no one is talking about Star. This silence gives the system a high surprise potential if a catalyst arrives.

near_term_catalysts: A successful completion of the block‑listing application, which would clear the way for a cleaner secondary market and could lift the share price.
Announcement of a new geothermal development in the Balkans or a partnership with a local grid operator.
A sudden rise in energy prices that makes geothermal projects more attractive to utilities.
A positive regulatory update from the UK or EU on renewable subsidies that could boost the company’s valuation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds the 3‑month average, a breakout above the current support level, and any new project approvals or partnership announcements.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score of 8.0 suggests a tight range, so a breakout could signal the start of the mean‑reversion phase. Confirmation will come from a sustained rally over 5–10 trading days.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Star’s deep crash has wiped out most of the weak hands, leaving a small, committed base of investors. If the share price recovers to half the 52‑week high, the upside could be roughly 70% from the current low. The company’s clean‑energy focus and recent capital raise provide a plausible catalyst for a rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s cash burn remains high, and it has sold a key asset, which could limit future upside. If regulatory approvals stall or new projects fail to materialise, the share could stay near the bottom or fall further. A worst‑case scenario sees the share stay flat or drop another 10–15% before any recovery.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is about 70% versus a downside risk of 30% if the share fails to rally. That’s a 2.3:1 reward‑to‑risk ratio, which is attractive for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 20‑day moving average and volume is at least 1.5× the 3‑month average. Use a 5‑day stop below the recent swing low. Allocate 5–10% of your swing‑trading capital to this position, adjusting size if you see a clear breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike that breaks the 20‑day MA or for a formal announcement of a new project. A price move above 25p (roughly the mid‑point of the 52‑week range) would be a clear trigger.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals. The only terminal risk is that the company fails to secure new projects or funding.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Star Energy sits at a deep crash level, has a quiet but potentially fertile environment for surprise, and offers a 70% upside against a 30% downside if a new project or regulatory boost arrives.

Historical data shows 2 rallies averaging 19% upside. Current position: -5.8%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
